programmes.show_description: Anna Richardson hosts the daring dating show as student Charles hopes to turn his disastrous love life around, while non-binary pansexual Lee is open to all genders.
Brand new series: Three new couples prepare for their first dinner party. But after the tension and strife of the last one, the atmosphere is far from welcoming.
The Goggleboxers have been watching: One Day, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Boarders, The Rescue and more.
When stunning Sasha is stood up, an unexpected knight in shining armour steps in: waiter Sam. Debbie's been married twice, but now says she's 'in the infancy of lesbianism'.
Mother Tucker: Cameron loves his mum, so Mitchell isn't sure how to tell Cam that she makes him uncomfortable. When Haley breaks up with Dylan, Phil takes it harder than anyone.
The One Under Edinburgh: Jack plans a haunting, horror-themed wedding, while his bride-to-be Becka dreams of a traditional Scottish affair. Will Jack's concept be a screaming success?
The Last Thanksgiving: Cameron and Mitchell's friends mistakenly assume they've split up - and they've definitely chosen a side. And Haley attempts to cook Thanksgiving dinner.
The Last Christmas: Cameron hopes to keep everyone happy for Christmas dinner, but Mitchell thinks he has an ulterior motive. Meanwhile, Claire tries to dodge Jay's job offer.
A short, cheeky clip featuring animals and food, from the always hilarious Tube that is Rude.
Charlie's: Gordon Ramsay visits Italian bistro Charlie's, where the pasta's soggy, the lasagne's a liability and the chef can't even cook a meatball.
Oates & Oates: When Ren decides to host an Earth Day telethon, Barry vows to help her book some A-listers. Beverly and her nemesis square off to find Bill a match.
The Fake-Up: As things get more serious between Adam and Brea, Beverly struggles to let go. Barry is thrown when Lainey Lewis comes back to town for a visit.
Family Portrait: It's family picture day and everything that can go wrong, does.
The Old Wagon: Claire finally gets Phil to agree to sell their old station wagon, but not before everyone takes it on one last ride down memory lane.
The Girl on the Bus: Lisa discovers the ideal family - and lies about her own.
I'm Dancing as Fat as I Can: Homer's broken promise infuriates Marge, so he studies ballroom dancing to try to win her back.
The Clown Stays in the Picture: Krusty reveals the story of the lost film that he made in the 80s with Homer and Marge.
A tattooist who loves to shoot, a gym bunny, a cheesemaker and a multilingual entrepreneur who loves a man in a suit are all looking for love on their first date.
Café Hon: The Café Hon restaurant in Baltimore is struggling because its owner has alienated the local community. Can Gordon help?
The Day the Earth Stood Cool: Homer persuades a hipster couple to move in next door, but their friendship soon starts to deteriorate...
To Cur with Love: Homer loses Santa's Little Helper but doesn't seem to care, until Grampa tells the story of Homer's long-lost childhood pup Bongo.
Dance Dance Revelation: Everyone gets in on the fun at Luke and Manny's first school dance. A trip to the mall with the boys becomes stressful for Jay and Phil.
Slow Down Your Neighbors: Claire becomes a determined neighbourhood vigilante hoping to catch and stop a menacing car that speeds through the streets.
The Girl on the Bus: Lisa discovers the ideal family - and lies about her own.
I'm Dancing as Fat as I Can: Homer's broken promise infuriates Marge, so he studies ballroom dancing to try to win her back.
The Clown Stays in the Picture: Krusty reveals the story of the lost film that he made in the 80s with Homer and Marge.

programmes.show_name: New: Dance Moms: A New Era
programmes.show_description: Brand new series - Dragged into the Drama: The girls tackle a glammed-up group dance portraying drag queens. But rumours of a dramatic return for Audrey make everyone wig out.
Pulpit Friction: A trendy new reverend recruits Homer as a deacon. Dry cleaners accidentally swap Marge's wedding dress for one of Krusty's clown costumes.
The Fabulous Faker Boy: Bart doesn't want to go to piano lessons, until he meets his beautiful teacher... Homer loses the last two hairs on his head.
Pixelated and Afraid: Homer and Marge's romantic getaway gets all Bear Grylls when they end up stranded in a sprawling forest being stalked by a wolverine.
Strong Arms of the Ma: When Marge is mugged, she reacts by working out with weights, but goes too far and ends up as a steroid-crazed bully.
Rebecca loves swimming with sharks. Dominic can't swim, but will he take the plunge with her? And how will musician James react to singer Brocarde's history of paranormal romance?
Thorpe Park: After passing his driving test, Simon takes the boys on their first road trip: a day out at Thorpe Park, where Will disgraces himself on the roller coaster.
Will Gets a Girlfriend: Will sees that going to a mixed-sex school isn't so bad when he pulls the most popular girl in the year above. But he alienates his hard-won new friends.
Caravan Club: The boys call Jay's bluff and go to the Camber Sands meeting of the Caravan Club to see if it really is a massive sex party. Jay's dad gives them advice about sex.
Xmas Party: It's the end of term and Will has been voted chairman of the Christmas prom committee, so he ropes in Simon, Jay and Neil to help get things organised.
(2014) Comedy sequel. The lads visit Jay in Australia after he tells them he's living a life of hedonism and sexual promiscuity as a superstar DJ.
Zoe is struggling to find someone who shares her enthusiasm for the natural look. Derry is worried that his jet-set lifestyle is ruining his chances of finding love.
Three daters return to the restaurant hoping for better luck second time around, while some couples who had great dates return - and one of them gets down on bended knee...
Thorpe Park: After passing his driving test, Simon takes the boys on their first road trip: a day out at Thorpe Park, where Will disgraces himself on the roller coaster.
Will Gets a Girlfriend: Will sees that going to a mixed-sex school isn't so bad when he pulls the most popular girl in the year above. But he alienates his hard-won new friends.
Island Time: For spring break, Barry talks Erica, Ren and the JTP into a discount vacation. Beverly worries when Adam considers taking time off after school.
The Return of the Formica King: Murray's nemesis 'Formica' Mike tries to buy Ottoman Empire.
The Big Ibizan Zoo One: Sonny is a party fanatic. How will his bride Jazmin greet his plans for a zoo-themed Ibizan beach party wedding with everyone covered in animal-print body paint?
The Grand Finale: It's time for the final. The three best Grand Master teams have 28 hours to showcase their skills with their most impressive builds yet.
Brand new series: In Kerala, the teams tackle tiger stripes, stick strikes and tuk-tuks, before tucking into a bounty of bananas! But a careless mistake could cost one duo dearly.
Brand new series: Jazz decides it's time to come clean about her feelings, but how will Inga react? Philip ramps up his feud with Chuggs. And Freddy's torn between Angus and Jack.
Sebastian's: Gordon Ramsay visits Sebastian's, a pizzeria in Los Angeles. The customers are scarce and the debt is mounting. Can Gordon turn the restaurant's fortunes around?
Finn McCool's: Gordon Ramsay visits Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, where the family feuding has got out of hand. Can Gordon mediate?
Brand new series: The second Commitment Ceremony erupts with accusations, heartbreak and a shock exit, leaving the experiment permanently shaken.
Brand new series: Two new couples join the experiment. But when big personalities clash, the marriages threaten to unravel before the honeymoons have even begun...
...for Stand Up To Cancer: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aléshé make decorated traybakes and tarte tatin, and recreate childhood toys in biscuits.
(2000) Tom Hanks and Tim Allen return in toy form for more endearing animated adventures. Buzz rallies his troops to foil a dastardly collector's plan to put Woody in a box.
Lisa the Skeptic: During a school archaeological dig, Lisa finds the skeleton of an angel on the site where a new shopping mall is to be built.
Realty Bites: Marge takes a job with Lionel Hutz's estate agency, but begins to question her morals when she has to try to sell the infamous Murder House to Ned Flanders.
Miracle on Evergreen Terrace: When Bart destroys the family's Christmas tree and presents, his lies about a burglar stealing them spiral out of control.
All Singing, All Dancing: The Simpsons remember all the great songs from their eventful lives after renting the movie Paint Your Wagon. With the guest voice of George Harrison.
Bart Carny: Homer and Bart start working at the fairground, but because of Homer's stupidity, a fair worker and his son lose their jobs. So they steal the Simpsons' house.
Strong Arms of the Ma: When Marge is mugged, she reacts by working out with weights, but goes too far and ends up as a steroid-crazed bully.
The celebs spend time on the sofa with George Clarke's Amazing Spaces, Swamp People: Serpent Invasion, The Woman in Black and the general election.
With The Celebrity Traitors, Strictly, Bake Off, Coronation Street, Married at First Sight UK. The Assembly, All's Fair and Songs of Praise.
